qualifying_org_definition: Non-profit or public organization serving health, social service, environmental protection, education, urban/rural development, recreation, public facilities, public safety, or childcare — per OTDA 25-ADM-03. In NYC (HRA), volunteer activities must be 'approved by HRA'; community-based organizations are routed through a CBO Provider Inquiry Form into either the Community Service Provider (CSP) or Self-Enrolled Voluntary Service Provider (SEVSP) track, implying pre-registration is required for NYC. Statewide OTDA policy (25-ADM-03) permits self-initiated placements at any qualifying nonprofit without district pre-approval outside NYC.
verification_instrument: Monthly ABAWD Volunteer Participation Record (OTDA 25-INF-05-Attachment-5; also issued as Monroe County ABAWD Monthly Volunteer Record Oct 2025). Completed by nonprofit staff, submitted by recipient to local social services district by the 10th of the following month.
hours_formula_detail: SNAP benefit ÷ higher of federal or state minimum wage for the recipient's area. NYC minimum wage is $17.00/hr; upstate is $15.50/hr. The Monroe County form instructions state: 'To find out how many hours that you need to volunteer each month, divide your monthly SNAP benefit by the current New York State minimum wage for your area.' Example from form: $155 benefit ÷ $15.50 = 10 hours/month. Representative minimum for a low-allotment household; max-allotment household (~$291 individual) ÷ $15.50 = ~19 hours upstate.
accepts_outofstate_detail: Neither OTDA 25-ADM-03 nor the Monroe County ABAWD Monthly Volunteer Record form specifies any geographic requirement for the certifying nonprofit. The form (Part 2) asks only whether the organization is 'public or non-profit' — no in-state registration or NY location requirement appears. NYC HRA's CSP/SEVSP provider inquiry process does not explicitly restrict to in-state organizations. Absent any state restriction, the federal default (any qualifying nonprofit) applies operationally. Policy is silent — out-of-state nonprofits like Tended (CA 501(c)(3)) appear eligible to sign the form, though NYC's provider pre-registration hoop remains a practical barrier.
geographic_waivers: None — fully enforced statewide as of March 1, 2026. OTDA Facebook posts confirm 'SNAP ABAWD work rules changed on March 1, 2026 for all counties in New York State.' Previous statewide waivers expired. The Tuscarora Nation Reservation (Niagara County) was noted in one source as a limited geographic exemption but this pertains to individual exemptions (federal Indian tribal land provision), not a county-level administrative waiver.
submission_deadline: By the 10th of the month following the month(s) of participation. The Monroe County form instructions state: 'You must give this completed form to your local social services district by the 10th of the month following the month(s) that you participate in the community service program.'
notes:
New York is Tier 2 due to a mandatory provider pre-registration hoop, especially in NYC where HRA requires volunteer sites to be 'approved by HRA' and routes community-based organizations through a CBO Provider Inquiry Form (CSP or SEVSP tracks) before recipient hours count. Upstate counties follow OTDA's framework, which permits self-initiated placements at any qualifying nonprofit using the Monthly ABAWD Volunteer Participation Record form submitted by the 10th of the following month — somewhat lighter, but still requires the county to accept/process the form on a rolling basis. County variation is significant: NYC HRA's pre-registration requirement makes it the strictest implementation; upstate counties applying OTDA guidance are closer to Tier 1 in practice but the state framework still requires district submission. ABAWD enforcement begins March 1, 2026 under HR1/OBBBA.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted four additional targeted searches and multiple page fetches against OTDA directives, NYC HRA, Legal Aid NYC, Legal Services NYC, snapguideny.org, City Limits, and Food Bank for NYC. Key findings: (1) REMOTE STANCE RESOLVED — ACCESS NYC FAQ explicitly states 'Remote work and in-person work both count toward your 80-hour monthly requirement,' confirming remote_stance='allowed'. (2) SELF-INITIATED PLACEMENT CONFIRMED FROM DIRECTIVE — search results surfaced the precise OTDA 25-ADM-03 policy language: 'Clients may self-initiate their placement in a voluntary community service activity or may be referred by the district.' This confirms upstate counties do NOT require organization pre-registration; the self-initiation right is codified in the governing OTDA directive. (3) NYC HRA APPOINTMENT NOT A UNIVERSAL GATE — The NYC PACE appointment (Pathways for Access to Careers and Employment) is sent to recipients 'not meeting requirements'; recipients who self-report as already volunteering report to HRA rather than being required to wait for an appointment before they can begin. This means the PACE appointment is not a mandatory prestep blocking the start of volunteering — it is a compliance-tracking mechanism. However, NYC's 'approved by HRA' language for volunteer activities remains: organizations that are not on the HRA radar must go through the CBO Provider Inquiry Form (CSP or SEVSP track) for their hosted hours to reliably count. (4) NO EDP REQUIRED — No source mentions an employability development plan or formal EDP as a gate before recipients can volunteer. (5) TIER 2 CONFIRMED — The NYC provider_registration hoop is real and documented from HRA's own pages, affecting the state's largest SNAP population. required_prestep='provider_registration' is accurate for NYC; upstate operates closer to Tier 1 in practice. Confidence raised to 'high': the OTDA directive language directly resolves the self-initiated placement question, the remote stance is explicitly confirmed from a primary government source (ACCESS NYC), and the NYC-specific provider hoop is documented from HRA's own materials. No material fields remain unknown after this research pass.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): Added five new dimension groups. (1) HOURS FORMULA — NY uses the workfare formula, NOT the federal 80-hour default. The Monroe County ABAWD Monthly Volunteer Record (Oct 2025) form instructions state explicitly: 'divide your monthly SNAP benefit by the current New York State minimum wage for your area.' NYC uses $17.00/hr; upstate uses $15.50/hr. Example given: $155 ÷ $15.50 = 10 hours/month. monthly_hours_required set to 10 as a representative low-allotment figure; actual hours vary by household benefit. (2) OUT-OF-STATE NONPROFIT — Policy is silent. Neither OTDA 25-ADM-03 nor the volunteer participation record form impose any geographic or in-state registration requirement on the certifying nonprofit. The form Part 2 asks only 'public or non-profit?' — no NY location required. Operationally the federal default (any qualifying nonprofit) applies; Tended as a CA 501(c)(3) appears eligible upstate but NYC's provider pre-registration hoop is still a practical barrier. (3) GEOGRAPHIC WAIVERS — None. OTDA confirmed March 1, 2026 enforcement for all counties statewide. The Tuscarora Nation Reservation note appears to be a federal individual exemption, not a county-level administrative waiver. (4) SUBMISSION UX — Paper form to local social services district by the 10th of the following month. No online portal or mobile upload identified. Cadence is monthly. (5) FORM FIELDS — Extracted directly from the Monroe County PDF (text successfully extracted via pypdf). Part 1 (recipient): name, case#, county, address, participant signature/authorization. Part 2 (org): program name, org name, org address, public/nonprofit checkbox, program start date, report month/year, current participation (yes/no), expected completion date, hours by month/year, staff signature, printed staff name, telephone, staff title. No EIN, no recipient DOB, no notarization required.
